# terrible_community_general_dnsimple (Data Source)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this data source against

### Optional

- `account_api_token` (String) Account API token. See `account_email` for more information.
- `account_email` (String) Account email. If omitted, the environment variables `DNSIMPLE_EMAIL` and `DNSIMPLE_API_TOKEN` are looked for. If those variables are not found, a `.dnsimple` file is looked for, see: https://github.com/mikemaccana/dnsimple-python#getting-started. `.dnsimple` config files are only supported in dnsimple-python<2.0.0.
- `domain` (String) Domain to work with. Can be the domain name (for example `mydomain.com`) or the numeric ID of the domain in DNSimple. If omitted, a list of domains is returned. If domain is present but the domain does not exist, it is created.
- `priority` (Number) Record priority.
- `record` (String) Record to add, if blank a record for the domain is created, supports the wildcard (*).
- `record_ids` (String) List of records to ensure they either exist or do not exist.
- `sandbox` (Boolean) Use the DNSimple sandbox environment. Requires a dedicated account in the dnsimple sandbox environment. Check https://developer.dnsimple.com/sandbox/ for more information.
- `solo` (Boolean) Whether the record should be the only one for that record type and record name. Only use with `state` is set to `present` on a record.
- `state` (String) Whether the record should exist or not.
- `ttl` (Number) The TTL to give the new record in seconds.
- `type` (String) The type of DNS record to create.
- `value` (String) Record value. Must be specified when trying to ensure a record exists.
